text.title: Brand Positioning Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Brand Positioning` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Target customer description MUST narrow to (a) buying authority + (b) trigger event + (c) firmographic OR demographic profile. 'Marketers' is forbidden; 'B2B SaaS heads of growth at companies in series-A with churn pain' is acceptable.
rule.rationale: Broad customer descriptions = broad messaging = no resonance. Narrower-than-comfortable forces real choices and produces messaging that converts.

rule.statement: MUST map the buyer's actual alternatives (including 'do nothing', spreadsheets, internal tools). Not just direct competitor list.

rationale.source: April Dunford positioning framework; Bob Moesta JTBD

rule.rationale: Buyers compare to alternatives, not just competitors. 'Do nothing' is the largest competitor for most products; missing it means missing the actual buying decision.

rule.statement: Positioning MUST name ONE 'only we' differentiator (one capability buyer values that ONLY this product delivers). Multi-differentiator positioning is unfocused.

rationale.source: April Dunford; Geoffrey Moore Crossing the Chasm

rule.rationale: Single 'only we' is the wedge into buyer attention. Multiple = none stands out. Forces team to pick + invest in the one wedge.

rule.statement: Positioning MUST explicitly name the category (existing or new). 'A new way to X' without category label is a brand failure.

rationale.source: Geoffrey Moore category-design literature; Play Bigger

rule.rationale: Category is the box buyer puts you in. Without naming, buyer slots you into the wrong box (likely a worse one). Name = control of the slot.

rule.statement: Positioning statement MUST be validated through >= 10 customer interviews before public launch. Pure-internal positioning is forbidden.

rationale.source: April Dunford 10-interview rule; B2B research handbook

rule.rationale: Internal positioning reads great in slides + flops with buyers. 10 structured interviews surface where words don't land + adjust.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
